pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 29-39 create students with grade_level='12'; export_result.sh lines 33-34 query WHERE grade_level='12'; task.json description states 'Senior class (Grade 12)'
setup_task.sh lines 42-48 create students with grade_level='9'; export_result.sh lines 37-38 query WHERE grade_level='9'

setup_task.sh line 25 creates attendance code with title='Excused'; export_result.sh line 41 queries WHERE title='Excused'; OpenSIS supports 'Excused Absence' as standard code (https://support.os4ed.com/hc/en-us/articles/201007427-How-Why-to-set-up-Attendance-Codes)

setup_task.sh line 25 creates code with short_name='E'; export_result.sh line 41 queries WHERE short_name='E'; OpenSIS database schema confirms short_name column in attendance_codes table (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OS4ED/openSIS-Classic/master/install/OpensisSchemaMysqlInc.sql)
setup_task.sh line 56 uses CURDATE(); export_result.sh line 57 uses CURDATE(); task.json description says 'for today's date'

privileged_info_summary: OpenSIS is a real, open-source Student Information System (GPL-licensed, OS4ED product) with full attendance tracking capabilities. The task uses Grade 12 as the target group and Grade 9 as a control group. The attendance status is 'Excused' with short code 'E', which is stored in OpenSIS's attendance_codes database table. All verification is performed against today's date using CURDATE(). The student data, attendance codes, and enrollments are synthetically generated during task setup via SQL INSERT statements, not from external sources. The task setup creates exactly 3 Grade 12 students (sen1, sen2, sen3) and 2 Grade 9 students (fresh1, fresh2) for testing purposes.
